title: Uniform Mixing in Chiral Quantum Walks
authors: Luke Levine, Jessy Jacob Mesapam, Benjamin Mustico, Christino Tamon, Gabriel Tucker, Hanmeng Zhan
abstract: This paper studies uniform mixing in continuous-time quantum walks. We show that for some unitary signing $σ$ the complete graph $K^σ_n$ has probabilistic uniform mixing. In contrast, it is known {\em no} complete graph has uniform mixing except for $K_2$, $K_3$, and $K_4$. Our technique is based on a stopping rule for quantum walks which reduces global to local uniform mixing. As a special case, we found an orientation of $H(n,4)$ that mixes to uniform faster than any other Hamming graphs. We also show that there are infinite families of oriented circulants with {\em average} uniform mixing. This is a chiral violation of Godsil's {\em No-Go} theorem which states that no graph has average uniform mixing except for $K_2$.
summary: This paper demonstrates that by using oriented (chiral) graphs, one can achieve uniform mixing in quantum walks where it was previously thought impossible. It provides new mathematical tools to reduce global mixing analysis to local measurements and identifies specific families of graphs that violate classical 'no-go' theorems.
main_problem: The paper investigates the conditions for uniform mixing and average uniform mixing in continuous-time quantum walks, specifically looking to overcome existing 'no-go' theorems by using chiral (oriented) graphs.
main_result: The authors prove that signed complete graphs can achieve probabilistic uniform mixing and that certain oriented circulants exhibit average uniform mixing, violating Godsil's no-go theorem.
method: The study employs unitary signings, complex equitable partitions, and a stopping rule based on weak measurements to reduce global mixing problems to local ones.
model_or_system: The model consists of continuous-time quantum walks on graphs (including complete, Hamming, and circulant graphs) where the Hamiltonian is defined by the adjacency matrix, potentially with complex edge weights (chiral/oriented graphs).
key_observables: Uniform mixing, average uniform mixing (Cesaro limit of the mixing matrix), and mixing time.
important_parameters: Unitary signing (sigma), vertex multiplicity, and the time (t) at which the distribution becomes uniform.
main_assumptions: The study assumes the use of complex edge weights (unitary signing) to bypass restrictions present in standard undirected graphs; it also notes a limitation regarding non-abelian groups due to irreducible representation degrees.
figures_summary: Figure 1 is a comparison table of quantum uniform mixing, and Figure 5 shows small examples of circulant graphs exhibiting average uniform mixing.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of uniform mixing, presents a reduction technique via a stopping rule, analyzes specific graph families (complete, Hamming, and circulant graphs), and concludes with a no-go theorem for non-abelian Cayley graphs.
why_interesting: This is interesting for researchers in quantum information and open quantum systems because it provides new methods for controlling state distributions and explores how broken symmetry (chirality) can be used to achieve mixing properties impossible in symmetric systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
